preamble: The alveolar nasal [n] shifts to the voiceless alveolar sibilant [s], a process often involving both the loss of voicing and the replacement of nasal closure with a narrow sibilant constriction.

examples.note: Common reduction of nasal-sibilant clusters leading to compensatory lengthening or simple sibilantization.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
